title: eupatorium beneolens f. typicum
intro: Eupatorium beneolens f. typicum, also known as Eupatorium capillifolium, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is often referred to as a type of boneset or white snakeroot. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. Herbal preparations of Eupatorium beneolens f. typicum can be used to relieve fever, headaches, and sore throats, as well as to support immune function and reduce pain and inflammation.
